commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ef8404ab3f046738f97ece5d132591f4b997e76c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` and its tests so generated-media direct fallback can run after an active requester wake fails while still preserving the steered active-session path when the wake succeeds.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection and the PR's focused mock path: current main blocks generated-media direct fallback whenever the requester is active, even after the active wake fails. I did not run a current-main failing test because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer real behavior proof and a small docs update before merge; no safe ClawSweeper repair can supply the contributor's environment proof.

target.title: fix: fallback after active media wake failure
target.body:
## Summary

- Follow up the superseded broader fallback PR #84371 with the narrowed remaining behavior gap only.
- Allow generated-media direct fallback after an active requester wake is attempted and fails, while preserving the active-session path when the wake succeeds.
- Keep the #84006 canonical behavior: fallback sends only media URLs still missing from message-tool delivery.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` -> 2 files, 114 tests passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated-media completion fallback now covers the active-session failure path left after #84006. If OpenClaw first tries to wake an active requester session, that wake fails, and the requester-agent handoff misses some generated media, OpenClaw sends one idempotent direct fallback containing only the still-missing media URLs. This is the focused follow-up requested after https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84371 was closed as overlapping the canonical #84006 fix.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/media-active-wake-fallback`, based on `origin/main` `57178b188bd`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`, `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

Evidence after fix: The new regression in `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` simulates an active requester wake that fails first with `transcript_commit_wait_unsupported` and then `no_active_run`, followed by requester-agent evidence that only `/tmp/generated-robot-1.png` was sent through the message tool. The test asserts direct fallback delivers `/tmp/generated-robot-2.png` only, using idempotency key `announce-channel-media-active-wake-failed:generated-media-direct`.

Observed result after fix: The focused Vitest run passed with `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 114 passed (114)`. Existing active wake success coverage still asserts no requester-agent call and no direct `sendMessage`, so the patch only opens fallback after the active wake has actually failed.

What was not tested: No live Discord/Slack/Telegram transport run was performed for this tiny follow-up. The existing #84006 proof covered the canonical generated-media fallback transport behavior; this patch only changes the internal branch that decides whether the already-canonical missing-media fallback may run after active wake failure.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
